perturbation.original_text: Term 4. The duration of this Venture (the "Term") will begin on March 1, 2020 and continue in full force and effect until February 28, 2025 or as otherwise provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Term 4. The duration of this Venture (the "Term") will begin on March 1, 2020 and continue in full force and effect until February 28, 2023 or as otherwise provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by shortening the term of the joint venture to February 28, 2023, conflicting with Section 36a which states that 'the Term expires and is not extended' is a condition for dissolution, while Section 5 states that 'The Term may be extended with the unanimous consent of all Members'. This creates uncertainty regarding the actual duration of the Venture and conditions under which it can be dissolved or extended. The contradiction introduces ambiguity about whether the agreement automatically dissolves in 2023, or continues until 2025 unless all members agree to end it sooner.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dissolution of the Joint Venture 36. The Venture will be dissolved and its assets liquidated in the event of any of the following: a. the Term expires and is not extended; b. a unanimous vote by the Members to dissolve the Venture; c. on satisfaction of the Purpose; d. loss or incapacity through any means of substantially all of the Venture's assets; or e. where only one Member remains.
perturbation.justification: The change in Term 4 shortens the agreement's duration from February 28, 2025, to February 28, 2023. This directly contradicts Section 36a, which states that the Venture will be dissolved when 'the Term expires and is not extended'. The modified Term 4 introduces a potential early dissolution date (2023), conflicting with the original intention that the venture would last until 2025 unless otherwise agreed, as indicated by the provision in Term 4: 'or as otherwise provided in this Agreement' and the extension provision in Section 5, causing in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Each of the Members has contributed to the capital of the Venture, in cash or property in agreed upon value, as follows (the "Capital Contribution"): Member Contribution Description Agreed Value BorrowMoney.com, inc 1, 500 square feet of Leased/rent office Space Includingspecified Description of duty* $3,500,000.00 USD JVLS, LLC dba Vaccines 2Go $60,000.00 USD From Monthly Government , City And State, And Or Private Awarded Contracts. Plus (10%) Of Any Generated Gross Revenue, In Add i t i on to The To ta l Contributions. $3,500,000.00 USD
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Each of the Members has contributed to the capital of the Venture, in cash or property in agreed upon value, as follows (the "Capital Contribution"): Member Contribution Description Agreed Value BorrowMoney.com, inc 1, 500 square feet of Leased/rent office Space Includingspecified Description of duty* $3,500,000.00 USD JVLS, LLC dba Vaccines 2Go $60,000.00 USD From Monthly Government , City And State, And Or Private Awarded Contracts. Plus (10%) Of Any Generated Gross Revenue, In Add i t i on to The To ta l Contributions. $350,000.00 USD
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the Agreed Value of JVLS, LLC's Capital Contribution to $350,000.00 USD, while the original agreement states it as $3,500,000.00 USD. This directly contradicts Section 38, which stipulates that distribution of amounts to Members upon liquidation will be made in proportion to their respective capital accounts. The reduced capital contribution of JVLS creates uncertainty about its share of assets during liquidation and affects the 'Valuation of Interest' defined in section 39. This leads to a conflict on how the distribution to JVLS would occur, based on the original amount or the modified amount.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 38. On the liquidation of the Venture assets, distribution of any amounts to Members will be made in proportion to their respective capital accounts or as otherwise may be agreed in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Agreed Value' of JVLS, LLC's capital contribution from $3,500,000.00 USD to $350,000.00 USD. This directly contradicts Section 38, which dictates that asset distribution upon liquidation will be proportional to each member's capital accounts. The significant reduction in JVLS's capital contribution creates a clear inconsistency in how liquidation proceeds would be distributed, directly conflicting with the proportionality requirement outlined in Section 38.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Within the limits of the Purpose of the Venture and the terms of this Agreement, the Managers, acting jointly, will have full authority to bind the Members in all matters relating to the direction, control and management of the Venture. Conduct and actions of the Managers will be dictated by policy and procedure established by the Members. Authority to bind the Venture in contract or in any third party business relation lies exclusively with the Managers, acting jointly.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Within the limits of the Purpose of the Venture and the terms of this Agreement, the Managers, acting independently, will have full authority to bind the Members in all matters relating to the direction, control and management of the Venture. Conduct and actions of the Managers will be dictated by policy and procedure established by the Members. Authority to bind the Venture in contract or in any third party business relation lies exclusively with the Managers, acting jointly.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction. Originally, Section 10 requires the Managers to act jointly to bind the Members. The modification now states that the Managers, acting independently, have full authority to bind the members, while retaining that authority to bind the venture in contract or in any third party business relation, lies exclusively with the Managers acting jointly. The contradictory statements create an uncertainty whether the managers can act individually for the direction, control, and management of the venture, or if they need to act jointly to bind the members. This contradicts itself as it states in the same section that they can act independently, while also stating that they must act jointly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Within the limits of the Purpose of the Venture and the terms of this Agreement, the Managers, acting independently, will have full authority to bind the Members in all matters relating to the direction, control and management of the Venture. Conduct and actions of the Managers will be dictated by policy and procedure established by the Members. Authority to bind the Venture in contract or in any third party business relation lies exclusively with the Managers, acting jointly.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Managers can act independently to bind the Members while simultaneously stating that they must act jointly to bind the Venture in contract or in any third-party business relation. This creates uncertainty whether the managers can act individually for the direction, control, and management of the venture, or if they need to act jointly to bind the members. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating that the contradiction exists within the changed section itself. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
